terms.definition: A type of evergreen tree that has needle-like leaves instead of broad flat leaves. The little fir tree in the story is unhappy with its needles.
terms.exampleUsage: The little fir tree stood alone in the forest.

terms.definition: Thin, pointed leaves shaped like needles, found on fir trees and pine trees. They do not fall off easily.
terms.exampleUsage: The fir tree had needle-like leaves that stayed green all year.

terms.definition: Short and slightly turned up at the tip. Used in the poem to describe a type of nose.
terms.exampleUsage: Some people have a snub nose that turns up at the tip.

terms.definition: A strong feeling of wanting something to happen or wanting to have something.
terms.exampleUsage: The fir tree made a wish for golden leaves.

terms.definition: Without any covering; empty. When a tree has no leaves, it is called bare.
terms.exampleUsage: After the man took the gold leaves, the tree was left bare.

terms.definition: A word that names a person, place, animal, or thing. It is also called a naming word.
terms.exampleUsage: In the sentence 'The goat ate the leaves', the nouns are goat and leaves.

terms.definition: Happy and satisfied with what you have. The fir tree was finally content with its own leaves.
terms.exampleUsage: At last, the fir tree was content with its needle-like leaves.

terms.definition: Easy to break or damage. The glass leaves on the fir tree were fragile.
terms.exampleUsage: The glass leaves were fragile and broke when the wind blew.

terms.definition: Having a sharp or narrow tip. Used to describe a type of nose in the poem and needle-like leaves.
terms.exampleUsage: The fir tree's needles were thin and pointed.

terms.definition: Being the only one of its kind; special and different from everything else.
terms.exampleUsage: Every person's nose is unique and special.
